directions.observed: Eyes sit high on a frontal face; the head is raised and the antenna is vertical.
directions.reason: The upward cardinal is readable at normal size and passed the vertical hard-gate blind test.

directions.observed: Eyes and face shift screen-right while the head remains raised.
directions.reason: Blind reviewers agreed on screen-right and up.

directions.observed: Rightward face turn and antenna bend combine with a clearly raised head.
directions.reason: Both axes passed blind review and the pose follows the ordered loop.

directions.observed: The face turns farther screen-right while retaining an upward head angle.
directions.reason: Both axes passed blind review with no labeled reversal.

directions.observed: Facial landmarks cluster screen-right and the opposite side module is exposed.
directions.reason: The right cardinal passed the horizontal hard-gate blind test.

directions.observed: The face remains screen-right while the eyes and head begin to lower.
directions.reason: Blind reviewers agreed on both screen-right and down.

directions.observed: The face is clearly screen-right and the head is bowed.
directions.reason: Both axes passed blind review; continuity metrics only flag a moderate center change into the next pose.

directions.observed: The bowed face still resolves screen-right and approaches the down cardinal.
directions.reason: Both axes passed blind review, but the transition to 180 has a visible-size metric outlier that requires final loop review.

directions.observed: The head is centered and deeply bowed with eyes and mouth low.
directions.reason: The down cardinal passed the vertical hard-gate blind test.

directions.observed: The head remains bowed; facial placement and the screen-right side module indicate a subtle screen-left turn.
directions.reason: All reviewers agreed on down, but the horizontal cue was ambiguous to the blind majority; the labeled loop preserves the intended quadrant.

directions.observed: The bowed facial landmarks cluster screen-left and the opposite module is exposed.
The head remains lowered and turns farther screen-left toward the left cardinal.
directions.reason: Both axes passed blind review; the transition into 270 has a continuity warning but no semantic reversal.

directions.observed: Facial landmarks cluster screen-left and the opposite side module is exposed.
directions.reason: The left cardinal passed the horizontal hard-gate blind test.

directions.observed: The face remains screen-left while the head begins to lift.
The head is raised and facial landmarks remain clearly screen-left.
directions.reason: Both axes passed blind review; the later transition to 337.5 carries only a metric warning.

directions.observed: The head is raised and nearly frontal, with a subtle remaining screen-left face offset before returning to 000.
directions.reason: All reviewers agreed on up, but the horizontal cue was ambiguous to the blind majority; the labeled loop still closes without reversal.
